description: Payroll account similar to T-S Misc.8.100, App. B 41, partly in the same hand and noting mostly the same names and sums for the 28th, 29th, and 35th weeks of the liturgical year. Cash was given to persons from Ascalon and Mosul and to a devotee, nazir (Heb.). In the 29th week space was assigned to salaries, superscribed al-rusūm, but the space was left blank. The letter samekh evidently stands for Arabic sittun (60). (Information from Mediterranean Society, II, p. 451, App. B 42)
description_he: חשבון שכר דומה ל-T-S Misc.8.100, עבור שבועות 28, 29 ו-35 של שנת הליטורגיה
